seo.title: Trang Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Trang travel guide for 2026. 4-day itinerary, flights from Bangkok, Emerald Cave tips, dim sum breakfast guide, island transport, and budget breakdown.

intro: Trang is the Andaman coast's most authentic province — where the fishing community still outnumbers the tourists, where breakfast means a 6 AM queue at a Chinese shophouse for roast pork and dim sum, and where an 80-meter swim through a sea cave reveals a hidden lagoon that no road could ever reach. Four to five days is the sweet spot: enough time for city dim sum culture, the Emerald Cave, a Koh Kradan beach day, and cave kayaking — all without feeling rushed. This guide covers the practical details.

itinerary.title: 4 Days in Trang: Caves, Islands & Dim Sum

days.title: Trang City — Arrival & Dim Sum Initiation
activities: Arrive by overnight train from Bangkok (7 AM arrival at Trang station) or by flight via Trang Airport (TST)
Morning: Drop bags at hotel then head directly to Thanon Ratchadamnoen for the full dim sum breakfast experience — Ruanthai Dim Sum or Phong Ocha, open from 6 AM (100-200 THB for a full spread)
Mid-morning: Walk to the Kew Ong Ear Chinese Shrine (5 min from the dim sum street) — beautiful Hokkien architecture and incense atmosphere
Lunch: Khanom jeen (southern Thai rice noodle curry) from one of the shophouses near the train station (60-80 THB)
Afternoon: Arrange tomorrow's island tour through your hotel or a local operator — confirm departure times and transport to Ban Chao Mai pier
Evening: Trang Night Market on Thanon Wiset Kunthorn (6-10 PM) — buy khanom phing (Trang coconut cakes), grilled seafood, and southern Thai curries (200-400 THB for dinner)
Late evening: Explore Thanon Phra Ram VI for a nightcap at one of the small local bars

days.title: Emerald Cave (Koh Mook) & Koh Kradan — 3-Island Tour
activities: Early morning: Dim sum breakfast by 6:30 AM — the tour departs at 8 AM with hotel pickup
8 AM: Minivan or songthaew transfer to Ban Chao Mai pier (38 km, ~50 min)
9 AM: Speedboat departs for the 3-island tour — first stop Koh Cheuk for 30 min snorkeling stop
10-11 AM: Koh Mook Emerald Cave — swim through the dark tunnel to the hidden lagoon (bring waterproof torch)
Noon-2 PM: Koh Kradan for lunch at beach restaurant and snorkeling on the east reef
2-4 PM: Free beach time on Koh Kradan — arguably Thailand's best beach
4:30 PM: Speedboat return to Ban Chao Mai pier, minivan back to Trang City
Evening: Recovery dinner near the hotel — try steamed sea bass with lime and chili at the night market (150-200 THB)

days.title: Le Khaw Kob Cave & Mangrove Kayaking
activities: 7 AM: Dim sum breakfast (this is now a daily ritual, budget 100-150 THB)
9 AM: Rent a motorbike (200-300 THB/day) or hire a driver (600-800 THB/day) for the inland cave route
10 AM: Le Khaw Kob Cave, Huay Yod District (30 km north of Trang City) — flat-bottomed boat through the underground river and stalactite chambers (~2 hours, 200 THB)
Noon: Lunch in Huay Yod market town — excellent local rice and curry (60-80 THB)
2 PM: Drive to Ban Chao Mai (38 km south) for afternoon mangrove kayaking in Hat Chao Mai National Park (200 THB entrance + 300 THB kayak rental)
4-5 PM: Kayak through the tidal mangrove channels — monitor lizards, kingfishers, mudskipper fish
6 PM: Return to Trang City for dinner
Evening: Try Trang's Indian-Muslim cuisine near the train station — roti canai and mutton curry from the Malay-Muslim food stalls (80-120 THB)

days.title: Koh Libong Dugong Tour or Overnight Island Extension
activities: Option A — Koh Libong Dawn Dugong Tour: 6 AM breakfast then taxi or motorbike to Kantang pier (24 km south). 7 AM ferry to Koh Libong (30 min, ~100 THB). Dawn boat tour for dugong spotting in the seagrass beds (500-800 THB, 2-3 hours). Return to Trang for late lunch
Option A afternoon: Kantang town walk — colonial train station architecture, riverside fish market, Peranakan shophouses

Option B — Island Extension to Koh Mook overnight: Pack a small bag and speedboat to Koh Mook (1 hour from Ban Chao Mai). Afternoon Charlie Beach relaxation and village walk. Next morning 7 AM kayak to Emerald Cave for the dawn-light experience in complete solitude
Both options: Final dinner at a Trang City seafood restaurant — order the local southern specialty, pla kapong nueng manao (steamed sea bass with lime sauce, 250-350 THB for a whole fish)

transport.fromBangkok: Trang Airport (TST) has daily flights from Bangkok (AirAsia from Don Mueang, Nok Air) from 1,800 THB one-way (1.5 hours). Airport is 15 minutes from city center — taxi 250 THB, songthaew 50 THB. By train from Hua Lamphong Station: 14 hours overnight, 2nd class sleeper 600-900 THB (recommended — saves a hotel night and deposits you at Trang station for 7 AM dim sum). By bus from Bangkok's Southern Bus Terminal: 12-14 hours (from 500 THB). From Krabi: 1.5 hours by minivan (250 THB, departs frequently from Krabi Town). From Hat Yai: 2 hours by bus or minivan (180-250 THB). From Satun (Koh Lipe access): 2 hours by minivan (250 THB).
localTransport: Motorbike rental: 200-300 THB/day — best for reaching Ban Chao Mai pier, Le Khaw Kob Cave, and Kantang. Ask your guesthouse for trusted rental shops
Songthaew (shared trucks): Run between Trang City and surrounding districts for 40-100 THB. Not always reliable for tourist attractions — check frequency before depending on them
Motorbike taxis: Available around the train station for 40-80 THB for city trips. Negotiate before boarding
Private car hire: 800-1,200 THB/day including driver — the most flexible option for visiting multiple inland attractions in one day
Ban Chao Mai speedboats: Depart from Ban Chao Mai pier, 38 km south of Trang City. Most tour operators include minivan transfer from city hotels. 3-island speedboat tours ~1,500-2,000 THB per person
Kantang ferry: Regular ferry to Koh Libong from Kantang pier, 24 km south of Trang City (~100 THB one-way)
Grab: Does not reliably operate in Trang — use motorbike taxis or arrange private drivers through your hotel

mustTry: Dim sum (Trang style) — Hokkien-Chinese bamboo-steamer dumplings unique to Trang: ha gao, siu mai, char siu bao, and egg tarts, served at Chinese shophouses from 5 AM (15-25 THB per piece, full breakfast 100-200 THB)
Moo yang (Trang roast pork) — whole pig marinated 8 hours in five-spice and Chinese herbs, slow-roasted to crispy perfection. Served over rice or alongside dim sum (80-120 THB per portion)
Kam chueang — Trang's unique coral-red dipping sauce made from cassava, vinegar, salt, and sugar. Every restaurant has their own recipe. Ask for it alongside your dim sum
Khanom phing (Trang cake) — small crispy coconut-egg pastry cakes cooked in brass mold pans, sold on the street from 5-6 THB each. A uniquely Trang street food
Khanom jeen tua khao — southern Thai fermented rice noodles served with a peanut and coconut curry sauce. Trang's local version is drier and nuttier than the Krabi version (60-80 THB)
Pla kapong nueng manao — steamed sea bass with lime juice and chili, the definitive southern Thai fish dish. Always fresh in Trang Province (200-350 THB for a whole fish)
Roti kanai — Indian-Muslim flatbread served with mutton curry or sweet condensed milk. Trang has a significant Malay-Muslim community near the train station (30-50 THB)
foodAreas: Thanon Ratchadamnoen (dim sum strip) — the legendary 5-6 AM breakfast street with 10+ shophouses serving dim sum and roast pork. Ruanthai Dim Sum and Phong Ocha are the most famous
Trang Night Market, Thanon Wiset Kunthorn — nightly (6-10 PM) southern Thai street food at local prices: khanom phing, grilled seafood, curries, fresh fruit
Train station area — Malay-Muslim food stalls around the train station serve roti canai, mutton curry, and nasi goreng, particularly busy from 7-11 AM
Kantang fish market (24 km south) — buy direct from boats in the early morning. Grilled fresh catch at the waterfront restaurants for 100-200 THB
tips: Dim sum stalls sell out early — arriving after 10 AM means the best pieces are gone. Set your alarm and go at 6-7 AM
The kam chueang dipping sauce is unique to Trang — don't leave without trying every restaurant's version
Southern Thai food is significantly spicier than central Thai — order 'mai pet' (not spicy) if you're sensitive to heat
The Muslim food stalls near the train station have exceptional value breakfasts from 7 AM — roti canai with curry for 40 THB
Island restaurants are convenient but expensive — everything comes by boat. Eat the big meals in Trang City and snack simply on the islands

etiquette: Dress modestly outside tourist areas — Trang has a significant Muslim population, particularly in fishing villages and on the islands. Cover shoulders and knees in villages
Remove shoes before entering mosques, temples, and many traditional homes
Ask permission before photographing the dim sum vendors and fishing communities — Trang's residents are not accustomed to being tourist subjects
The Vegetarian Festival (October, 9th lunar month) involves intense religious ceremonies — observe respectfully and follow any instructions from organizers
Tipping is appreciated but not expected: 20-50 THB at restaurants, 50-100 THB for boat drivers and guides, 100-200 THB for cave tour guides
Stand during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public spaces including train stations and parks

budget.budget: 700-1,400 THB/day (~$20-39 USD): City guesthouse 400-700 THB, dim sum breakfast 100-150 THB, local meals 50-100 THB, island day tour including transport 1,500-2,000 THB (averaged over a 5-day trip). Trang is cheaper than Krabi by 30-40% for accommodation and food. The main cost is island transport rather than daily living.
budget.midRange: 2,500-5,000 THB/day (~$70-140 USD): Rua Rasada Hotel 2,000-3,500 THB, restaurant meals 300-600 THB, organized island tours 1,500-2,000 THB, motorbike rental 200-300 THB. The sweet spot for a comfortable Trang trip with flexibility for cave tours and island hopping.
budget.luxury: 6,000-15,000+ THB/day (~$168-420+ USD): Anantara Si Kao Resort 5,500-12,000 THB, Mook Lamai Spa Resort 3,500-7,000 THB, private speedboat charters 3,000-6,000 THB, fine dining 1,500-2,500 THB. Trang's luxury ceiling is significantly lower than Krabi's (no Ritz-Carlton equivalent) but the Anantara delivers genuine 5-star quality in a national park setting.
